title: Odisha government on Tuesday announced the names of three senior IAS officers to hold talks with Maoist negotiators for release of two Italians held hostage by the extremists for the last six days in the deep forests of Kandhamal district. The Maoists had kidnapped Bosusco Paolo and Clavdio Colangelo on March 14 and followed it up with 13 demands for the government to accept for their release. - The Times of India
